candidates.description: The road surface shows multiple patch repairs and utility covers throughout the intersection. Image 3 (South) shows a large rectangular concrete patch/manhole cover in the foreground with a pedestrian walking across. The pavement has various filled repairs and utility access points but no active potholes with missing asphalt visible.

The intersection shows a well-maintained urban road with a central landscaped median featuring grass and plantings. A circular manhole cover is visible in the center of the roadway in image 2. The road surface is asphalt in generally good condition with some normal surface wear and minor discoloration but no potholes or significant damage.

Multiple utility covers and patch repairs visible in the road surface at an intersection. The road shows rectangular concrete/metal utility access covers and patched areas around them. No actual potholes with missing asphalt are present - the visible features are all infrastructure elements (manholes, utility covers) and their associated repair patches.

The location shows a construction/development area with visible drainage infrastructure. In Image 3 (East), there is a metal drainage grate/manhole cover visible on the sidewalk near the curb edge. The road surface itself shows normal wear with some weathering but no actual potholes or missing asphalt. A construction site with fencing is visible to the north.

debug_points.description: The road surface shows multiple patch repairs and utility covers throughout the intersection. Image 3 (South) shows a large rectangular concrete patch/manhole cover in the foreground with a pedestrian walking across. The pavement has various filled repairs and utility access points but no active potholes with missing asphalt visible.

The intersection shows a well-maintained urban road with a central landscaped median featuring grass and plantings. A circular manhole cover is visible in the center of the roadway in image 2. The road surface is asphalt in generally good condition with some normal surface wear and minor discoloration but no potholes or significant damage.

Multiple utility covers and patch repairs visible in the road surface at an intersection. The road shows rectangular concrete/metal utility access covers and patched areas around them. No actual potholes with missing asphalt are present - the visible features are all infrastructure elements (manholes, utility covers) and their associated repair patches.

The road surface shows general wear with some uneven patches and minor surface deterioration. There are small depressions and rough texture visible in the asphalt, but no distinct potholes with missing material or exposed base layer. The pavement appears weathered with some discoloration and minor surface irregularities.

The location shows a construction/development area with visible drainage infrastructure. In Image 3 (East), there is a metal drainage grate/manhole cover visible on the sidewalk near the curb edge. The road surface itself shows normal wear with some weathering but no actual potholes or missing asphalt. A construction site with fencing is visible to the north.

The road surface shows general wear and deterioration typical of aging asphalt. There are some uneven patches, minor surface irregularities, and wear marks visible, but no discrete holes where asphalt material is physically missing. The pavement appears weathered with some discoloration but maintains structural integrity.

The road surface shows general wear and weathering with some uneven texture at the transition between the paved sidewalk area and the street. There is minor surface deterioration and water pooling in low spots, but no discrete holes with missing asphalt are visible. The pavement appears aged but intact.
